quickFacts: At noon, two mechanical goats butt heads on the Town Hall clock tower
St. Martin's croissants (rogale świętomarcińskie) are a protected regional treat
Craft beer scene is excellent — look for Pinta and Trzech Kumpli
One of Poland's oldest cities and its first capital
foodScene: Rogale świętomarcińskie (St. Martin's croissants) are a must — flaky pastry with white poppy seed filling. Pyzy (potato dumplings) are the local comfort food. Hit Stary Browar for upscale dining or the milk bars for budget Polish classics under €3.

places.name: Old Market Square
places.caption: Colourful Renaissance gem
places.description: One of Europe's most beautiful squares. Pastel merchant houses, buzzing café terraces, and the iconic Town Hall with its goat clock.

places.name: Cathedral Island (Ostrów Tumski)
places.caption: Birthplace of Poland
places.description: The oldest part of the city on a tiny island. The cathedral crypt holds tombs of Poland's first rulers. Eerily atmospheric at twilight.

places.name: Stary Browar
places.caption: Brewery turned art mall
places.description: A 19th-century brewery converted into an award-winning shopping and arts centre. Art installations sit between shops — genuinely worth visiting.

places.name: Citadel Park
places.caption: Green escape with history
places.description: A huge park built on a former Prussian fortress. War memorials, a military museum, and locals jogging and picnicking everywhere.

places.name: Śródka District
places.caption: Street art neighbourhood
places.description: A once-forgotten district reborn through massive murals and quirky cafés. The trompe-l'oeil mural on Śródka is stunning.

places.name: Croissant Museum
places.caption: Bake your own rogal
places.description: Interactive museum where you learn the history of St. Martin's croissants and bake your own. Genuinely fun and delicious.

places.name: Lake Malta
places.caption: Urban recreation lake
places.description: Rowing, a summer ski slope, a mini zoo, and waterside cafés. Locals flock here on warm days. Rent a kayak or just chill.

places.name: Palm House (Palmiarnia)
places.caption: Tropical escape in Poland
places.description: One of the largest palm houses in Europe with 17,000 plants. The fish ponds and jungle atmosphere are surprisingly impressive.

places.name: Imperial Castle
places.caption: Controversial landmark
places.description: Built for Kaiser Wilhelm II, now a cultural centre with exhibitions and events. The history is complex and worth reading about.

places.name: Day Trip: Kórnik Castle
places.caption: Fairy-tale castle & arboretum
places.description: A neo-Gothic island castle surrounded by Poland's largest arboretum. Just 20km south of Poznań — perfect half-day trip.

places.name: Palmiarnia (Palm House)
places.caption: Tropical greenhouse
places.description: One of Europe's largest palm houses with 17,000 plants, tropical fish aquarium and a viewing platform. In Wilson Park.

places.name: Rogalin Palace
places.caption: Baroque palace & ancient oaks
places.description: 18th-century palace 20 km south with 1,000-year-old oak trees — Lech, Czech and Rus. Painting gallery and French gardens.

places.name: National Museum in Poznań
places.caption: Art & history
places.description: Paintings by Malczewski, Matejko and European masters. The Gallery of Polish Medieval Art has stunning Gothic altarpieces.

places.name: Street Art in Śródka
places.caption: Mural neighbourhood
places.description: Neighbourhood across the Cybina river with large-scale murals, including the famous 3D trompe-l'oeil mural of old Śródka.
